mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative SPX 20-day returns in >= 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over three months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in >= 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a demand-supply expansion loop that amplifies revenue growth forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: Joint increases in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to generate positive SPX 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term contributes less than 1% incremental R-squared over main effects in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, month-over-month growth in retail sales is interpreted as a transient policy-dependent effect rather than organic demand, causing equity markets to discount the signal and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, month-over-month increases in RSAFS are followed by positive SPX 5-day returns in > 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An annualized three-month change in consumer prices exceeding 3.0% signals mounting inflation pressure that raises the probability of future monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3.0%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in >= 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects accelerating residential investment and construction employment that feeds into broader cyclical earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST are followed by negative SPX 5-day returns in >= 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll increase exceeding 200 thousand persons signals robust labor market momentum that boosts consumer spending forecasts and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand are followed by negative SPX 20-day returns in >= 45% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and consumer prices indicate a restrictive monetary response to inflation that compresses discount rates and profit margins jointly, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint monthly increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are not followed by negative SPX 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, and the interaction term lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.
